title: Auth Service Degraded — EU-West Session Cache Exhaustion — RESOLVED
rca: Refresh token rotation feature (deployed 23:45 UTC) stored token pairs with a 24-hour TTL, causing Redis in eu-west-1 to accumulate double the normal session data over 12 hours of EU-West traffic. At 92% Redis memory utilization, LRU eviction began removing active sessions, producing 4-6s auth latency and session invalidations for ~30% of EU-West users.
resolution_steps: 1. Emergency Redis flush initiated at 11:23 UTC — expired rotation tokens cleared
3. Hotfix deployed: rotation TTL reduced from 24h to 1h (PR #8821)
4. Added token rotation memory testing to pre-deploy checklist
business_impact: 31-minute degradation impacting ~30% of EU-West users (~2,400 sessions). 3 enterprise accounts experienced Salesforce SSO disruption. No data loss. No US-region impact. All accounts restored.

communication_log.note: Root cause identified, Redis flush initiated
Flush complete, hotfix deployed — incident resolved
All enterprise accounts confirmed restored — ticket closed
